items.custom_prompt: Enter the complete standard reference(s) used for this evaluation including title, number, edition, and date. Format: 'Standard Number: Standard Title, Edition/Date' (e.g., 'UL 508A: Industrial Control Panels, 3rd Edition 2013' or 'IEC 60950-1: Information Technology Equipment - Safety, 2nd Edition 2005'). If multiple standards, list each on a new line

Specify which stage(s) of the evaluation process identified the non-conformances. Choose: 'during our evaluation (Stage 1)', 'during our testing (Stage 2)', or 'during our evaluation (Stage 1) and testing (Stage 2)'

Specify the type of deliverable document that will be provided. Choose: 'CB Test Report' for CB scheme, 'ETL CDR' (Construction Details Report) for ETL listing, or specify another deliverable type (e.g., 'Field Evaluation Report', 'Technical Assessment')

items.key: <will be sent under separate cover/attached to this letter>
items.value: <will be sent under separate cover/attached to this letter>
items.custom_prompt: Specify how the draft deliverable will be provided. Choose: 'will be sent under separate cover' if sending separately, or 'attached to this letter' if including with this letter report

Specify what is being evaluated. Choose the most appropriate: 'product' for a single item, 'products' for multiple items, 'system' for an integrated system, or specify another appropriate term (e.g., 'equipment', 'assembly', 'installation')

Specify what type of samples or documentation are needed. Choose: 'additional' if new samples/documents are required, 'modified' if changes to existing samples/documents are needed, or 'additional/modified' if both may apply

items.key: the intrinsic safety analysis and constructional evaluation
items.value: the intrinsic safety analysis and constructional evaluation

items.custom_prompt: Specify when the non-conformances were identified. If found during initial documentation review, keep as 'Initial Documentation Review'. If found during another phase, replace with the appropriate phase name (e.g., 'Physical Inspection', 'Performance Testing', 'Final Evaluation')

value.Requirement of the Clause: Required equipment markings must be present and visible from the exterior or after opening, and not on removable parts.
Required equipment markings must be visible and use correct symbols.
value.Remark and Findings: No required markings or symbols present on equipment.

value.Requirement of the Clause: Equipment must be identified by model number, name, or other means.
Equipment shall be marked with rated voltage, current, and frequency.
value.Remark and Findings: No electrical ratings marked on the equipment.

value.Requirement of the Clause: Where necessary for safety, indication of purpose of terminals, connectors, controls and indicators must be marked.
Gas and liquid connections must be clearly marked near the connector with means of identifying the gas or liquid, maximum permitted pressure, and flow direction if applicable.
value.Remark and Findings: No identification, maximum pressure, or flow direction marking provided on the equipment.

value.Requirement of the Clause: Equipment must be accompanied by documentation for safety purposes for operator or responsible body.
value.Remark and Findings: No such document provided with equipment.

value.Requirement of the Clause: Documentation must include information specified in 5.4.2 to 5.4.6.
Documentation must include instructions for lifting and carrying if equipment is over 18 kg.
value.Remark and Findings: No instructions for lifting or carrying.

value.Requirement of the Clause: Documentation must include supply voltage or voltage range, frequency or frequency range, and power or current rating.
value.Remark and Findings: Incomplete/insufficient electrical ratings provided in IFU.

value.Requirement of the Clause: Documentation must include description of all input and output connections.
Documentation must include statement of the range of environmental conditions, including mains supply voltage fluctuations, overvoltage category, wet location, and pollution degree.
value.Remark and Findings: None provided for mains supply voltage fluctuations, overvoltage category, wet location, and pollution degree.

value.Requirement of the Clause: Documentation must include instructions for assembly, location, and mounting requirements.

Documentation must include identification and description of operating controls, positioning for disconnection, specification of intermittent operation limits, explanation of symbols used, listing of any poisonous or injurious gases and quantities, risk reduction procedures relating burn from surfaces permitted to exceed limits, additional precautions for IEC 60950 conforming equipment in regard to moistures and liquids, and a statement about protection impairment if used in a manner not specified by the manufacturer.

Documentation must include instructions sufficient in detail permitting safe maintenance and inspection and continued safety, instruction against the use of detachable mains supply cord with inadequate rating, rating and characteristics of fuses, product specific risks may affect service personnel, protective measures for these risks, and verification of the safe state after repair.
Equipment over 18 kg must have means for lifting or carrying, or directions given in documentation.
value.Remark and Findings: No means for lifting or carrying and no directions given in IFU.

items.custom_prompt: Specify when the non-conformances were identified. If found during initial documentation review, keep as 'Initial Documentation Review'. If found during another phase, replace with the appropriate phase name (e.g., 'Physical Inspection', 'Performance Testing', 'Final Evaluation')

Specify when the non-conformances were identified. If found during initial documentation review, keep as 'Initial Documentation Review'. If found during another phase, replace with the appropriate phase name (e.g., 'Physical Inspection', 'Performance Testing', 'Final Evaluation')
Extract the project number from the document. This is typically found in the header or introductory section and follows a format like 'Project No. XXXXX'
Determine if repeat testing is required based on the evaluation results. Fill with 'and conduct repeat testing, if required' if testing issues were identified, otherwise leave blank
Select 'will' if modified samples or additional documentation WILL be required for follow-up project based on the non-conformances identified

items.custom_prompt: Extract the title/role of the reviewer from the document. Common titles include 'Senior Engineer', 'Project Manager', 'Technical Reviewer', etc.
